# UX-NNNN — <Title>

<!-- Header: the document title line in the form "UX-NNNN — <Title>". -->

## Metadata
<!-- Metadata: the document's identifying fields; fill each value when the spec is created. -->

- **UX ID:** UX-NNNN
- **Title:** <one line: the screen or flow name>
- **Status:** <one line: current status value, per the documentation status definitions>
- **Version:** <one line: current version, per the versioning rules>
- **Scope level:** UX behaviour (non-visual, non-technical)

## Disclaimer
<!-- Disclaimer: the standard statement every UX Specification carries; kept at the top for consistency with existing specs. -->

> One line: this document describes user, screen, and interaction behaviour only — not visual design (colors, typography, spacing, icons, animations, components) and not implementation (HTML, CSS, frameworks, APIs, database, backend, frontend).

## Non-Goals
<!-- Non-Goals: what a UX Specification as a document type is NOT for; distinct from a screen's Section 4 "Out of Scope". -->

- <one line: not a visual design document>
- <one line: not an implementation or technology decision>
- <one line: not a product decision (behaviour traces to approved PRDs)>

---

## Required Sections
<!-- Required Sections: the 19 sections below are mandatory; keep them in order and include no UI design examples. -->

## 1. Purpose
<!-- One line: what this screen or flow is for. -->

## 2. Business Value
<!-- One line: why it matters, tied to the Foundation principles. -->

## 3. Scope
<!-- One line: what this screen or flow includes. -->

## 4. Out of Scope
<!-- One line: what this specific screen excludes, including V2/excluded items. -->

## 5. Entry Points
<!-- One line: how a person arrives at this screen or flow. -->

## 6. Exit Points
<!-- One line: where a person can go from here. -->

## 7. Screen Overview
<!-- One line: the parts of the screen described in behaviour terms, not visuals. -->

## 8. Screen Behaviour
<!-- One line: how the screen behaves as a person interacts with it. -->

## 9. User Actions
<!-- One line: the actions a person can take. -->

## 10. System Responses
<!-- One line: how the system responds to each action, in behaviour terms. -->

## 11. Validation Behaviour (Product level)
<!-- One line: product-level validation rules; TODO where undefined. -->

## 12. Empty States
<!-- One line: behaviour when there is nothing to show. -->

## 13. Loading States
<!-- One line: behaviour while content is being retrieved. -->

## 14. Error States
<!-- One line: behaviour when something fails. -->

## 15. Permissions
<!-- One line: what each role can do here, referencing the Identity PRD. -->

## 16. Accessibility Notes
<!-- One line: product-level accessibility expectations (operable, perceivable); no visual specifics. -->

## 17. Related PRDs
<!-- One line: the PRDs this spec realizes. -->

## 18. Acceptance Criteria
<!-- One line: Gherkin scenarios (Given / When / Then) for the behaviour. -->

## 19. Open Questions (TODO only)
<!-- One line: TODO items only. -->

---

_Template only. Replace placeholders and one-line explanations with real content when creating a UX Specification; keep all 19 sections and add no UI design examples here._
